---
title: "Lecture 17: DOM Manipulation & Array Methods"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 23, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 17]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[DOM Manipulation & Array Methods]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 5 | Unit II: DOM Manipulation<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Select DOM elements with modern selectors
- Modify element content, attributes, and styles
- Create and remove elements dynamically
- Use array methods to transform data
- Combine DOM manipulation with array operations
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="What is DOM"}

[What is the DOM?]{.slide-title}

<hr>

The **Document Object Model** is a tree-like representation of HTML.

::: {.body-text-m}
- Every HTML tag becomes a **node**
- JavaScript can read and modify nodes
- Changes to DOM update the page live
:::

```javascript
document // root object
  └─ document.body
      └─ child elements
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Selecting Elements"}

[Selecting Elements]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
document.getElementById('header');
document.querySelector('.navbar');
document.querySelectorAll('p');
document.getElementsByClassName('item');
```

::: {.body-text-m}
- `querySelector` returns first match
- `querySelectorAll` returns all matches (NodeList)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Reading Content"}

[Reading and Modifying Content]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
const para = document.querySelector('p');

console.log(para.textContent);  // text only
console.log(para.innerHTML);    // HTML markup
para.innerHTML = "<strong>Updated!</strong>";
```

- `textContent`: plain text
- `innerHTML`: HTML tags included

---

## {data-menu-title="Modifying Attributes"}

[Modifying Attributes]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
const img = document.querySelector('img');

img.setAttribute('src', 'photo.jpg');
img.alt = 'Profile picture';
console.log(img.getAttribute('src'));
```

Attributes control element behavior and display.

---

## {data-menu-title="Styles"}

[Modifying Styles]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
const box = document.querySelector('.box');

box.style.color = 'blue';
box.style.backgroundColor = 'lightgray';
box.style.fontSize = '18px';
```

::: {.body-text-m}
CSS property names become camelCase in JavaScript.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="classList"}

[Working with Classes]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5|6"}
const btn = document.querySelector('button');

btn.classList.add('active');
btn.classList.remove('disabled');
btn.classList.toggle('highlight');
console.log(btn.classList.contains('active'));
```

`classList` is cleaner than manipulating `className` directly.

---

## {data-menu-title="Creating Elements"}

[Creating New Elements]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5|6"}
const newDiv = document.createElement('div');
newDiv.textContent = 'Hello from JS!';
newDiv.classList.add('message');

const parent = document.querySelector('#container');
parent.appendChild(newDiv);
```

Dynamically build UI from JavaScript.

---

## {data-menu-title="Removing Elements"}

[Removing Elements]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
const item = document.querySelector('.old-item');
item.remove();

// OR using parent
parent.removeChild(item);
```

Use `.remove()` for simpler code.

---

## {data-menu-title="Array map"}

[Array Method: map()]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
const nums = [1, 2, 3, 4];
const doubled = nums.map(x => x * 2);
console.log(doubled); // [2, 4, 6, 8]
```

::: {.body-text-m}
`map()` transforms each element and returns a new array.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Array filter"}

[Array Method: filter()]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
const numbers = [5, 12, 8, 130, 44];
const large = numbers.filter(n => n > 10);
console.log(large); // [12, 130, 44]
```

::: {.body-text-m}
`filter()` selects elements that pass a test.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Array reduce"}

[Array Method: reduce()]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
const values = [10, 20, 30];
const sum = values.reduce((acc, val) => acc + val, 0);
console.log(sum); // 60
```

::: {.body-text-m}
`reduce()` combines all elements into a single value.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Array find"}

[Array Methods: find() & includes()]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
const users = [{id: 1}, {id: 2}, {id: 3}];
const user = users.find(u => u.id === 2);

const fruits = ['apple', 'banana', 'mango'];
console.log(fruits.includes('banana')); // true
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Combining"}

[Combining DOM & Arrays]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5|6|7"}
const students = ['Asha', 'Vikram', 'Neha'];
const list = document.querySelector('#student-list');

students.forEach(name => {
  const li = document.createElement('li');
  li.textContent = name;
  list.appendChild(li);
});
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Practice"}

[Practice Task]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
Given an array of product prices, create a `<ul>` with one `<li>` per product showing: "Price: ₹X"

Use `map()` or `forEach()` to build the list dynamically.
:::

```javascript
const prices = [100, 250, 450];
```

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 18 - Form Validation
</div>
</div>
